title: SamaVaani: Auditing and Debiasing Multilingual Clinical ASR for Indian Languages
abstract: arXiv:2606.26901v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) is increasingly used to document clinical encounters, yet its reliability in multilingual and demographically diverse Indian healthcare context remains largely unknown. In this study, we first conduct the systematic audit of ASR performance on real-world psychiatric interview data spanning Kannada, Hindi and Indian English, comparing eight state-of-the-art models including IndicWhisper, WhisperLargeV3, Sarvam, GoogleS2T, Gemma3n, OmniLingual, Vaani, and Gemini. Our results reveal substantial variability across models and languages, with some systems performing competitively in Indian English but failing in regional speech. We further fine-tune two of the best performing opensource models, i.e., Gemma3n and OmniLingual, using various methods. With this, we uncover systematic performance gaps tied to speaker role and gender, raising concerns about equitable deployment in clinical settings, which are fur

Title: SamaVaani: Auditing and Debiasing Multilingual Clinical ASR for Indian Languages
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26901v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) is increasingly used to document clinical encounters, yet its reliability in multilingual and demographically diverse Indian healthcare context remains largely unknown. In this study, we first conduct the systematic audit of ASR performance on real-world psychiatric interview data spanning Kannada, Hindi and Indian English, comparing eight state-of-the-art models including IndicWhisper, WhisperLargeV3, Sarvam, GoogleS2T, Gemma3n, OmniLingual, Vaani, and Gemini. Our results reveal substantial variability across models and languages, with some systems performing competitively in Indian English but failing in regional speech. We further fine-tune two of the best performing opensource models, i.e., Gemma3n and OmniLingual, using various methods. translated_title: SamaVaani：面向印度语言的多语言临床自动语音识别系统的审计与去偏研究
translated_abstract: 自动语音识别技术越来越多地被用于记录临床诊疗过程，但其在印度多语言和人口多样化医疗背景下的可靠性仍基本未知。本研究首先对覆盖卡纳达语、印地语和印度英语的真实世界精神病学访谈数据进行了系统的ASR性能审计，比较了包括IndicWhisper、WhisperLargeV3、Sarvam、GoogleS2T、Gemma3n、OmniLingual、Vaani和Gemini在内的八种最先进模型。我们的结果揭示了不同模型和语言之间的显著差异，一些系统在印度英语上表现优异，但在地区性语言中表现失败。我们进一步使用多种方法对两个表现最佳的开源模型（即Gemma3n和OmniLingual）进行了微调。通过此举，我们发现了与说话者角色和性别相关的系统性性能差距，引发了关于在临床环境中公平部署的担忧。
tldr: 本研究系统审计了八种主流ASR模型在印度多语言临床场景下的性能，发现模型间和语言间存在显著差异，并揭示出说话者角色和性别导致的系统性性能偏差，通过微调开源模型初步探索了去偏方法。
en_tdlr: This study systematically audits eight state-of-the-art ASR models on multilingual clinical data from India, revealing substantial performance variability across models and languages, and uncovers systematic biases related to speaker role and gender, with initial debiasing efforts through fine-tuning open-source models.
